Developer_Error_Message: 400 Bad Request: This error code indicates that the request was not properly formed or contained invalid data or Parameter
401 Unauthorized: This error code indicates that the requester is not authenticated or authorized to access the requested resource.
403 Forbidden: This error code indicates that the requester is authenticated but does not have sufficient permissions to access the requested resource.
404 Not Found: This error code indicates that the requested resource was not found on the server.
405 Method Not Allowed: This error code indicates that the HTTP method used in the request is not allowed for the requested resource.
409 Conflict: This error code indicates that the requested action could not be completed due to a conflict with the current state of the resource.
500 Internal Server Error: This error code indicates that an unexpected error occurred on the server while processing the request.
503 Service Unavailable: This error code indicates that the server is currently unavailable and cannot process the request.
429 Too Many Requests: This error code indicates that the requester has exceeded the allowed rate limit for accessing the requested resource.
201 This error occurs when you try to access a property of an undefined or null object.
203 Failed to load : This error occurs when an OpenLayers/Leflet component or external resource fails to load. This could be due to a network issue or a missing or invalid resource URL.
Business_Error_Message: Failed to load Map / External resource Librarys

Developer_Error_Message: 204 TypeError : This error occurs when you try to use a function or object that is not a constructor.
205 Projection code not found : This error occurs when you try to use a projection that is not defined in your proj4js configuration.
Business_Error_Message: Projection code not found in proj4js.defs

Developer_Error_Message: 300 Unsupported geometry type : This error occurs when you try to use a geometry type that is not supported by OpenLayers/Leflet.
